history_text: 47-year-old female with a 3-year history of Type 2 diabetes mellitus, currently poorly controlled (HbA1c 9.1%). Comorbidities include hypertension. Renal function is mildly to moderately reduced (eGFR 69.4 mL/min/1.73m²). Current medications include Metformin 500mg twice daily, Insulin glargine 20 units nightly, Amlodipine 5mg once daily. No known drug allergies. She denies recent hospitalisations or surgical procedures.
